item.title: EU MiCA enforcement began July 1, requiring all CASP operators to hold active authorisation or wind down EU client activities.
DTCC began live tokenized trades of Russell 1000 equities and Treasuries in a July pilot with BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, and 50+ firms.
A 140-firm consortium including Visa, Stripe, Mastercard, and BlackRock launched Open USD, a shared reserve-revenue dollar stablecoin.
SEC placed three crypto rulemakings on its July agenda covering token offerings, broker-dealer custody, and crypto trading venue rules.
Wanchain's Cardano-BNB Chain bridge was exploited July 21, with attackers stealing approximately $10 million in NIGHT tokens.
US agencies face a July 18 deadline to publish GENIUS Act stablecoin implementing rules, with regulations taking effect by January 2027.
Coinbase received UK investment services authorisation, enabling clients to trade crypto, derivatives, and equities on one platform.
California's Digital Financial Assets Law took effect July 1, requiring state licences for all crypto firms serving California residents.
EU Transfer of Funds Regulation dropped the Travel Rule threshold to zero, requiring identity data on all EEA stablecoin transfers.
BIS research found a $3.5 billion aggregate stablecoin inflow reduces 3-month Treasury bill yields by up to 4 basis points within 10 days.
H1 2026 crypto hacks totalled $972 million across 207 incidents; North Korea-linked groups accounted for roughly 66% of stolen funds.
DTCC announced Stellar blockchain integration for tokenized securities, with broader availability planned for the first half of 2027.
Coinbase enabled near-instant UCITS mutual fund entry and exit via stablecoin, a regulatory first for European regulated funds.
Corporate Bitcoin treasury holdings surpassed 750,000 BTC across at least 172 publicly traded companies by mid-2026.
SEC proposed a crypto token safe harbour covering issuers raising up to $75 million and early-stage projects valued under $5 million.
